messages.say:
ChatGPT-File Category Description.json


File


rewrite these instructions in .json and make them more understandable for ai. the attached file is the source. :


AI Execution Instructions: SSOT-Driven Refactor (Registry → IDs/Modules → Planning → Templates)


Authoritative inputs (do not invent)


Use these as ground truth for structure, classification signals, and current registry shape:


Final consolidated status + readiness context:

File classification signals + decision tree:

Unified registry (structure + column headers + file list):

Registry path update precedent (fields updated + backup behavior):

Existing registry sync utility behavior (patterns for layer/artifact_kind derivation):

Recursive file-id assignment utility (naming + exclusions + allocator usage):

Counter store format (do not mutate outside controlled runs):



0) Objective (what “done” means)


You will implement a deterministic, CLI-executable refactor driven ONLY by four SSOT artifacts:


MOVE_MAP.json

REFERENCE_INDEX.json

REWRITE_PATCHSET/ (rewrite plans)

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/ (proof: hashes + reports)


Acceptance outcome:


Registry updated first (module assignment points to destination dir_id).

Files moved second (filesystem matches MOVE_MAP).

Imports/paths rewritten third (codebase compiles/tests pass).

Evidence bundle proves: before/after hashes, registry diffs, rewrite diffs, move logs, validation results.



1) Hard rules (lockdown)


1.1 No implied behavior


If a transformation is not expressed in SSOT artifacts, do not perform it.


1.2 Two-phase apply


Phase A: “Plan + Patch” (generate SSOT, patch registry, validate; no file moves)

Phase B: “Move + Rewrite” (perform moves, apply rewrites, validate; no registry guessing)


1.3 Deterministic ordering


All lists MUST be processed in a stable order:


by file_id ascending, then

by source_relpath ascending.


1.4 Fail-closed + rollback-ready


If any validation fails, stop immediately and write:


EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/rollback_instructions.md

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/failure_fingerprint.json


1.5 Registry update policy


Follow the established pattern: update only specified fields, create backups, produce update counts (as in prior registry path update run).



2) Define the 4 SSOT artifacts (formal)


2.1 MOVE_MAP.json (single source of truth for relocation)


Purpose: declares the only allowed file relocations.


Minimum fields per record:


file_id (20-digit)

source_relpath

dest_dir_id (20-digit directory ID)

dest_dir_relpath (dir folder name including dir_id prefix)

dest_relpath (dest_dir_relpath + filename)

category (REGISTRY_FILES | ID_FILES | MULTI_CLI_PLANNING | TEMPLATE_OPERATIONS)

confidence (HIGH|MED|LOW)

match_signals[] (why it matched; deterministic strings)

move_enabled (bool; default false until validated)


Global fields:


schema_version

generated_utc

repo_root

dir_id_resolution block (how dest_dir_id was discovered)

counts_by_category


2.2 REFERENCE_INDEX.json (single source of truth for what must be rewritten)


Purpose: enumerates every reference that must change as a result of the move.


Minimum fields per reference record:


ref_id (stable hash of: file_id + old_text + new_text + location)

source_file_id

source_relpath

ref_type (PY_IMPORT | FROM_IMPORT | PATH_STRING | JSON_POINTER | YAML_PATH | MD_LINK | CLI_ARG | POWERSHELL_PATH | OTHER)

old_value

new_value

location (line/col or JSON pointer if structured)

rewrite_plan_id (links into REWRITE_PATCHSET)

confidence

evidence (snippet hash, not raw content)


2.3 REWRITE_PATCHSET/ (machine-executable rewrite plans)


Purpose: expresses how changes are applied, without performing them yet.


Directory layout:


REWRITE_PATCHSET/manifest.json

REWRITE_PATCHSET/plans/.json


Each plan must include:


rewrite_plan_id

target_file_id

target_relpath

strategy:


TEXT_REPLACE_SET (ordered replacements)

PY_IMPORT_REWRITE (AST-based if available; else deterministic regex set)

JSON_POINTER_PATCH (RFC-6902 ops)

pre_hash_sha256

post_hash_sha256_expected (optional if computed post-apply)

operations[] (explicit; no wildcards unless bounded + justified)

dry_run_output_path (where the diff preview will be written)


2.4 EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/ (proof of correctness)


Purpose: stores before/after proofs and run reports.


Required outputs:


EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/run_manifest.json (run_id, tool versions, timestamps)

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/before_snapshot/:


registry_hashes.json

file_hashes.json (sha256 for all moved + rewritten targets)

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/after_snapshot/ (same structure)

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/:


move_plan_report.md

registry_patch_report.md

rewrite_plan_report.md

rewrite_apply_report.md

validation_report.md

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/diffs/:


registry.patch.rfc6902.json

rewrite_diffs/ (unified diffs per file)

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/logs/:


move.log

rewrite.log

errors.jsonl



3) Concrete classification rules for the 4 target categories


Use directory signals + filename patterns + content heuristics. Prefer “directory signals” first (as in quick reference).


3.1 Category: REGISTRY_FILES


A file is REGISTRY_FILES if ANY are true:


Path contains a registry root folder (example: .../REGISTRY...) or filename contains registry AND artifact is structured config (.json/.yaml).

The file is the unified registry itself or any derived registry projections.

Strong indicator: registry schema/headers like column_headers, files[], entries[] (see unified registry structure).


3.2 Category: ID_FILES


A file is ID_FILES if ANY are true:


Name contains: ID_CANONICALITY, COUNTER_STORE, id_allocator, canonical_id_patterns, DIR_ID, FILE_ID.

It is part of ID allocation / ID validation / ID gates (as described in the ID system execution report patterns and scripts).


3.3 Category: MULTI_CLI_PLANNING


A file is MULTI_CLI_PLANNING if ANY are true:


Located under planning areas (LP_LONG_PLAN, PHASE_* planning folders, “phase contract”, “planning”, “execution plan”).

Contains terms indicating tool-to-tool orchestration: workstreams, gates, phase_contract, envelope_contract, mutation set, plan conversion, multi-cli, orchestrator.


3.4 Category: TEMPLATE_OPERATIONS


A file is TEMPLATE_OPERATIONS if ANY are true:


It is a template definition or template executor (filenames contain TEMPLATE, template, AUTONOMOUS_DELIVERY_TEMPLATE, “template operations”).

It performs structured patching / generation over canonical artifacts (e.g., apply patch atomic / patch plans).


3.5 Ambiguity rule (must be explicit)


If multiple categories match, pick ONE by precedence:


REGISTRY_FILES

ID_FILES

TEMPLATE_OPERATIONS

MULTI_CLI_PLANNING
Record all matches in match_signals[], but only one category.



4) Directory targets (module folders) and dir_id binding


4.1 You must resolve destination folders to real dir_ids


Do not fabricate dest_dir_id. Resolve by reading:


existing directory naming convention with dir_id prefixes (as used in path update run).

directory .dir_id metadata where present (DIR-ID governance system is production-ready).


4.2 “module_id in registry” update


Wherever the unified registry stores a module assignment (e.g., module_id, or equivalent field), you will set it to the destination folder’s dir_id. If the registry does not yet have module_id, you will:


add a new column/header in column_headers for module_dir_id (or your chosen canonical name), and

populate it for every file in MOVE_MAP.json.


All changes must be represented as an RFC-6902 patch and stored in evidence. (Pattern consistent with prior patch tracking in evidence).



5) CLI-executable end-to-end steps


Step 1 — Create a branch + run_id


Create a new git branch for this refactor.

Generate run_id (timestamp + random suffix) and store it in EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/run_manifest.json.


Step 2 — Build candidate inventory (no changes)


From repo root:


Load unified registry file and build a lookup: file_id -> current relative_path from files[].

Scan filesystem to confirm paths exist (report missing/stale).


Output:


EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/inventory_consistency_report.md

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/before_snapshot/file_hashes.json (only for candidate files)


Step 3 — Classify files into the 4 categories


Apply the concrete rules in §3 to every file in registry and/or filesystem.

Produce deterministic match signals.


Output:


MOVE_MAP.json with move_enabled=false for all records initially

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/classification_report.md


Step 4 — Resolve destination dirs + produce final MOVE_MAP


For each category, map to a destination “section folder” (one per category). For each folder:


resolve its dir_id

write dest_dir_id and dest_dir_relpath


Then compute each dest_relpath deterministically.


Flip move_enabled=true only if:


destination dir exists and has valid dir_id

source file exists

no dest path collision


Output:


updated MOVE_MAP.json

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/move_plan_report.md


Step 5 — Generate REFERENCE_INDEX (dry scan)


Scan all files that may reference moved files:


Python imports (import x, from x import y)

path strings referencing old relpaths

JSON/YAML references (known pointers, config fields)

markdown links


For each hit, compute deterministic new_value based on MOVE_MAP.


Output:


REFERENCE_INDEX.json


Step 6 — Generate REWRITE_PATCHSET (dry-run)


For each impacted file in REFERENCE_INDEX.json:


create a rewrite plan (REWRITE_PATCHSET/plans/.json)

compute pre_hash_sha256

write dry-run diffs (do not modify files yet)


Output:


REWRITE_PATCHSET/manifest.json

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/rewrite_plan_report.md


Step 7 — Patch registry FIRST (no file moves)


Create RFC-6902 patch that:


updates module_dir_id (or your canonical module field) for all moved files

optionally updates relative_path only if your registry policy requires it pre-move (recommended: keep current relpath until after move, then do a second patch; be explicit which policy you choose)


Apply patch, write backup, and write update counts (same style as registry path update report).


Output:


EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/diffs/registry.patch.rfc6902.json

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/registry_patch_report.md


Step 8 — Validate registry integrity (gates)


Run:


schema validation (registry schema rules)

uniqueness checks (file_id uniqueness, no duplicate paths)

module_dir_id populated for moved cohort


Output:


EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/validation_report.md (Phase A)


Step 9 — Move files (filesystem)


Execute moves strictly from MOVE_MAP.json where move_enabled=true.
Requirements:


log every move

verify dest exists

verify post-move sha256 equals pre-move sha256 (content unchanged)


Output:


EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/logs/move.log

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/after_snapshot/file_hashes.json (moved files)


Step 10 — Apply rewrites (imports/paths)


Apply REWRITE_PATCHSET plans:


enforce pre_hash_sha256 match (fail if drift)

apply operations

write unified diff per file

compute post-hash


Output:


EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/diffs/rewrite_diffs/*

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/rewrite_apply_report.md

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/logs/rewrite.log


Step 11 — Patch registry SECOND (final paths)


Now update registry relative_path/directory_path/absolute_path fields for moved files to match filesystem (exactly like the prior directory rename path update behavior).


Output:


EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/diffs/registry_paths_after_move.patch.rfc6902.json

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/registry_after_move_report.md


Step 12 — Full validation and tests


run unit/integration tests

run any gates that enforce import hygiene / canonicality


Output:


EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/validation_report.md (Phase B)

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/after_snapshot/registry_hashes.json


Step 13 — Commit set


Commit, in order:


SSOT artifacts (MOVE_MAP/REFERENCE_INDEX/REWRITE_PATCHSET)

registry patch(es)

moves + rewrites

evidence bundle (or store evidence outside git if policy says so; be explicit)



6) Required CLI commands (express as a CLI plan; do not handwave)


You will implement or invoke CLI commands that map 1:1 to the steps above, e.g.:


cli refactor plan --out MOVE_MAP.json --evidence EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/

cli refactor references --move-map MOVE_MAP.json --out REFERENCE_INDEX.json

cli refactor rewrite-plan --reference-index REFERENCE_INDEX.json --out REWRITE_PATCHSET/

cli registry patch --registry --patch <rfc6902.json> --backup

cli refactor move --move-map MOVE_MAP.json

cli refactor rewrite-apply --patchset REWRITE_PATCHSET/

cli validate all --evidence EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/


If these CLIs don’t exist yet, you must create minimal scripts that implement them with deterministic args and outputs; do not embed logic in ad-hoc manual commands.



7) Output checklist (must exist at end)


MOVE_MAP.json

REFERENCE_INDEX.json

REWRITE_PATCHSET/manifest.json + per-file plans

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/ with:


before/after hashes

registry patches (RFC-6902)

move + rewrite logs

validation reports



8) Non-negotiable behaviors


Never move a file not present in MOVE_MAP.json.

Never rewrite a file not present in REWRITE_PATCHSET/.

Never update registry fields not declared in the registry patch.

Always produce backups before registry writes (consistent with prior runs).

Always preserve file content during moves (hash equality check).



Updated AI Execution Instructions (apply these overrides to the prior instruction set)


A) Hard constraints (new, non-negotiable)


Only move files and folders that have IDs


A file is eligible to move only if it has a valid file_id (20-digit) in the unified registry AND/OR the filename is ID-prefixed (e.g., P_<20digits>_*).

A folder is eligible to move only if it has a valid dir_id (20-digit) assigned and the folder name is ID-prefixed (e.g., <20digits>_NAME).

Any file/folder lacking an ID is ineligible and must be reported as SKIPPED_NO_ID.


Human review required before any move


Before executing git mv / filesystem moves, AI must generate a single review document listing every planned move with the current complete file path (absolute path) for each file that will move.

AI must stop after producing this review artifact. No move execution occurs until a human approves.



B) Canonical destination folders (fixed targets)


All moved files MUST land in exactly one of these four destination roots (no new roots):


REGISTRY_FILES →
C:\Users\richg\Gov_Reg\01260207201000001250_REGISTRY

ID_FILES →
C:\Users\richg\Gov_Reg\01260207201000001162_GEU\01260207201000001165_ID

MULTI_CLI_PLANNING →
C:\Users\richg\Gov_Reg\01260207201000001221_Multi CLI

TEMPLATE_OPERATIONS →
C:\Users\richg\Gov_Reg\newPhasePlanProcess


Rules:


Do not change these paths.

Do not derive alternative destinations.

The dest_dir_id must be taken from the folder prefix in each destination path.



C) Required new artifact: Human Move Review Document


File name + location (deterministic)


Write to:
EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/HUMAN_MOVE_REVIEW.md


Required contents (must include)


For each move record, include:


file_id

category (one of the four)

current_absolute_path (FULL path)

current_relative_path (from C:\Users\richg\Gov_Reg)

destination_root_absolute_path (one of the four roots above)

destination_absolute_path (FULL path after move)

destination_relative_path

source_dir_id (if known)

dest_dir_id (must match destination root folder prefix)

move_enabled (true only if all eligibility checks pass)


Also include summary totals:


total candidates

total eligible

skipped (no file_id)

skipped (ambiguous category)

skipped (destination collision)


Stop condition


After writing HUMAN_MOVE_REVIEW.md, AI must:


set MOVE_MAP.json records to move_enabled=false by default

optionally produce a second file MOVE_MAP_APPROVAL.json placeholder

terminate execution (do not perform moves)



D) Updated MOVE_MAP requirements (destination mapping locked)


For each category, destination roots are fixed:


REGISTRY_FILES → 01260207201000001250_REGISTRY

ID_FILES → 01260207201000001165_ID

MULTI_CLI_PLANNING → 01260207201000001221_Multi CLI

TEMPLATE_OPERATIONS → newPhasePlanProcess (this one is not ID-prefixed; therefore only move items inside it that already have file_id/dir_id)


Special rule: newPhasePlanProcess root is not ID-prefixed


You may move files with file_id into that folder.

You may move folders with dir_id into that folder.

You must not move any non-ID items there “just because they belong.”



E) Updated execution sequence (inserted human gate)


Phase A — Plan + Patch (no moves)


Build classification + candidate list (unchanged)

Apply “only items with ID” eligibility filter (new)

Build MOVE_MAP.json with the locked destination roots (new)

Generate REFERENCE_INDEX.json and REWRITE_PATCHSET/ (unchanged)

Patch registry module fields (unchanged)


Phase A.5 — Human Review Gate (new)


Generate EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/HUMAN_MOVE_REVIEW.md (new)

STOP (no moves)


Phase B — Move + Rewrite (only after human approval)


After approval, set move_enabled=true for approved items only

Move files/folders (ID-only) strictly from MOVE_MAP

Apply rewrites from REWRITE_PATCHSET

Patch registry paths (post-move)

Validate, test, evidence



F) Additional validations (must run before HUMAN_MOVE_REVIEW is produced)


Existence check


Confirm each current_absolute_path exists.


Collision check


Confirm each destination_absolute_path does not exist (unless explicitly permitted; default is fail).


Registry alignment check


Confirm registry relative_path for file_id matches the filesystem (or emit as MISMATCH_REGISTRY_VS_FS and block move).


ID integrity check


file_id is exactly 20 digits.

dir_id is exactly 20 digits.



G) Update to “only move folders that have id”


When MOVE_MAP includes directories:


Add entity_type: "file"|"dir"

For dir entities, require:


folder name begins with the 20-digit dir_id

registry (or dir-id registry) confirms assignment

If either fails: SKIPPED_NO_DIR_ID



H) Required outputs (end of Phase A)


Must exist before stopping:


MOVE_MAP.json (with move_enabled=false by default)

REFERENCE_INDEX.json

REWRITE_PATCHSET/

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/HUMAN_MOVE_REVIEW.md

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/move_plan_report.md

EVIDENCE_BUNDLE/reports/registry_patch_report.md

Registry patch artifacts (RFC-6902)


No filesystem moves should have occurred yet.
